authoritative_sources.title: 21 CFR Part 1 Subpart O - Sanitary Transportation of Human and Animal Food
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: 21 CFR Part 1 Subpart O is the primary U.S. regulatory source for shipper, loader, carrier, and receiver responsibilities, including receiver assessment after temperature-controlled shipments, carrier pre-cooling, written procedures, training, and record retention.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Cold-chain transport prerequisite and evidence validation
used_for: shipper, carrier, loader, and receiver boundary handling

authoritative_sources.title: Frequently Asked Questions: FSMA Food Traceability Rule
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FDA FAQ is the current authoritative public explanation of covered-food traceability plans, record retrieval, key data expectations, and entity responsibility for maintaining traceability records.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Lot and shipment traceability evidence review
used_for: cold-chain handoff prerequisites for covered foods

authoritative_sources.title: 21 CFR Part 117 - Current Good Manufacturing Practice, Hazard Analysis, and Risk-Based Preventive Controls for Human Food
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: 21 CFR Part 117 is the primary public regulatory source for warehousing and distribution conditions, hazard analysis, monitoring records, corrective action, recall planning, and risk-based supply-chain program boundaries in food manufacturing.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Quality hold, release, records, and supplier-program boundary review
used_for: where coordination stops and formal quality or supplier-program ownership begins

authoritative_sources.title: Control of Listeria monocytogenes in Ready-To-Eat Foods: Guidance for Industry
authoritative_sources.source_class: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FDA guidance is the authoritative public operating reference for refrigerated RTE storage and transportation controls, including time-temperature limits, verification of control, pre-cooling, vehicle suitability, and hold or corrective-action handling when refrigerated controls fail.

authoritative_sources.title: EPCIS & CBV
authoritative_sources.source_class: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GS1 EPCIS and CBV are recognized public standards for event messaging, business-step semantics, disposition handling, and sensor-linked visibility data, including cold-chain monitoring across enterprises.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Cross-system event, sensor, and handoff normalization

authoritative_sources.title: GS1 Global Traceability Standard
authoritative_sources.source_class: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GS1 is the recognized public standards body for cross-enterprise traceability identifiers and minimum traceability requirements used across food manufacturing and temperature-controlled supply chains.
used_for: lot, pallet, shipment, and location identifier structure

authoritative_sources.title: SQF Food Safety Code - Storage and Distribution, Edition 10
authoritative_sources.source_class: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: SQF storage and distribution guidance is a recognized food-industry operating reference for documented temperature monitoring procedures, reaction plans, calibration, and records used in cold storage and distribution operations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Cold storage monitoring and reaction-plan packaging

workflow_stages.name: Dependency intake and prerequisite validation
workflow_stages.description: Capture the request, prior owner, next owner candidate, product and facility context, lot and shipment scope, temperature requirements, and tenant authority rules before any handoff or closure claim.
source_classes: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references

workflow_stages.name: Cross-team cold-chain coordination and routing
workflow_stages.description: Normalize system evidence and terminology, reconcile schedules and ownership, and prepare handoff or status outputs with explicit lot, shipment, temperature, and acceptance requirements.
source_classes: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references

workflow_stages.name: Traceability-aware excursion and exception handling
workflow_stages.description: Preserve lot, logger, trailer, temperature, inventory, hold, and supplier evidence when conflicts, excursions, holds, or substitution pressure appear so the lane escalates instead of guessing.
source_classes: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references

workflow_stages.name: Closure confirmation and orchestrator return
workflow_stages.description: Mark transfer complete only when the destination owner, required records, and blocker resolution are explicit; otherwise return safely for escalation.
source_classes: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references

artifacts: production orders, dock schedules, and service-level commitments
lot, batch, pallet, shipment, seal, and trailer records
temperature specifications, reefer setpoints, pre-cool confirmations, logger files, and excursion alerts
inventory states, cold-room locations, FEFO status, dwell-time data, and traceability logs
quality holds, release records, nonconformance reports, and corrective-action documentation
supplier records, carrier records, approved-supplier status, and destination acceptance records
handoff summaries, blocker logs, routing confirmations, and closure confirmations

allowed: classify requests as in-scope, blocked, or out-of-scope for cold-chain coordination
organize authoritative records, required artifacts, next owners, and blocker states for cold-chain handoffs
summarize temperature-control, traceability, inventory, supplier, and release dependencies without implying final execution or approval authority
escalate: quality hold, excursion disposition, release, recall, or nonconformance decisions requiring formal quality authority
supplier approval, substitute-material approval, carrier approval, or engineering or process-change authority
conflicting public and tenant policy that materially changes excursion, release, quarantine, or ownership handling
refuse: bypass traceability, temperature-control, release, or quality controls to preserve throughput or service levels
approve excursion-impacted, held, or unreleased product for shipment, movement, or use
treat supplier-temperature nonconformance or substitute-material approval as coordination-only when formal approval is required

domain_failure_modes: A handoff proceeds without authoritative lot, logger, release, or destination-owner evidence.
Conflicting ERP, MES, WMS, TMS, and logger records are collapsed into a single confident answer instead of a blocker.
A temperature excursion or quality hold is treated as a scheduling inconvenience rather than a formal stop condition.
Supplier substitution, alternate carrier approval, or nonconformance disposition is handled as routine coordination instead of routed to supplier-quality or quality owners.
The lane absorbs warehouse or logistics execution work instead of preserving coordination boundaries.
Closure is declared without proof that the right destination owner accepted the required artifact set.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Supplier or substitution decision outside lane authority
Work is handed off without required context or artifacts
Multiple teams appear to own the same step
The lane is asked to absorb execution work instead of coordinating it
Ownership is ambiguous across multiple teams
A handoff is attempted without the required context

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant-specific temperature specs and excursion thresholds
unresolved_ambiguities.detail: Public sources establish the need for temperature control, monitoring, and records, but product-family-specific setpoints, allowable excursion windows, and disposition rules remain tenant supplied at runtime.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Jurisdiction or customer-specific release and quarantine movement rules
unresolved_ambiguities.detail: Public FDA and recognized standards define baseline controls, but stricter customer contracts, export rules, or site SOPs may narrow movement or release options and must be retrieved rather than assumed.
